description: ol-not-enough-inputs (issue #111): a parenthesized under-supply of a strictly fixed-arity Core primitive. `first` has no variadic form (corePrimitiveArityRange gives min === max === 1), so its minimum is exact and `(first)` — zero inputs where exactly one is required — is a statically-known too-few (spec/tooling.md:181), pointing at the callee. Unlike bare calls (whose surplus becomes a parse-stage stray), the parenthesized form is where an over- or under-supply is explicit; the lower bound of a bounded-alternate/open-variadic primitive is instead left to the runtime arity check (#97), see arity-paren-variadic-ok.
